content:
# Plan and Implement

## Change Summary

Made `ProposeNewBead` reconciliation replay-safe and dependency-aware without restructuring existing flows. The milestone service now first reuses prior `ProposedBeadCreated` journal records, recovers crash-left same-title beads by matching the rendered proposal payload, injects the new bead as a downstream dependency on the active bead before syncing, records placement metadata in the journal, and reuses the same duplicate-eligibility filter when resolving created bead IDs. The success-reconciliation path now exercises that idempotent behavior on reruns, and the test suite covers journal replay, crash recovery, dependency repair, stricter fallback resolution, threshold logging, and end-to-end reconciliation replay.

## Steps

1. [Completed] Inspect the existing propose-new-bead reconciliation flow, duplicate checks, journal metadata, and replay behavior to isolate the three pending amendments without touching `.ralph-burning/` review scope.
2. [Completed] Update `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs` so propose-new-bead handling is idempotent via journal reuse, recovers matching previously created beads instead of misclassifying them as planned-elsewhere, injects the created bead dependency edge, records placement metadata, and reuses the duplicate eligibility filter in created-bead ID fallback resolution.
3. [Completed] Extend milestone-record tests to cover journal-based replay reuse, recovery of an already-created bead with dependency repair, dependency metadata/command emission, threshold logging, and fallback resolution that ignores closed or self matches.
4. [Completed] Update `src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs` regression coverage so replaying the same accepted propose-new-bead aggregate is idempotent and does not emit a spurious planned-elsewhere mapping.
5. [Completed] Run formatting, tests, linting, and the authoritative `nix build` gate on the final tree.

## Validation Evidence

- `cargo fmt` completed successfully.
- `cargo test` completed successfully on the modified tree.
- `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` completed successfully.
- `cargo fmt --check` completed successfully.
- `nix build` completed successfully.
- Targeted regressions passed for the propose-new-bead service tests and for `reconcile_proposed_beads_after_success_creates_follow_up_from_latest_aggregate` replay coverage.

## Outstanding Risks

- Duplicate suppression for genuinely new follow-up work is still title-based, so semantically duplicate findings with different proposed titles can still create separate beads.
- Crash recovery without an existing journal record relies on the recovered bead still matching the rendered propose-new-bead description; if that description was edited manually before replay, the handler will conservatively treat it as planned elsewhere instead of attributing it to the interrupted creation.
